children.heading: Services of Conveners and Facilitators.—
children.label: a Services of Conveners and Facilitators.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An agency may employ or enter into contracts for the services of an individual or organization to serve as a convener or facilitator for a negotiated rulemaking committee under this subchapter, or may use the services of a Government employee to act as a convener or a facilitator for such a committee.

children.heading: .—Determination of conflicting interests
children.label: 2 .—Determination of conflicting interests
children.text: An agency shall determine whether a person under consideration to serve as convener or facilitator of a committee under paragraph (1) has any financial or other interest that would preclude such person from serving in an impartial and independent manner.

children.heading: .—Services and Facilities of Other Entities
children.label: b .—Services and Facilities of Other Entities
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, an agency may use the services and facilities of other Federal agencies and public and private agencies and instrumentalities with the consent of such agencies and instrumentalities, and with or without reimbursement to such agencies and instrumentalities, and may accept voluntary and uncompensated services without regard to the provisions of . The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service may provide services and facilities, with or without reimbursement, to assist agencies under this subchapter, including furnishing conveners, facilitators, and training in negotiated rulemaking.section 1342 of title 31
such member certifies a lack of adequate financial resources to participate in the committee; and
the agency determines that such member’s participation in the committee is necessary to assure an adequate representation of the member’s interest.

children.heading: .—Status of Member as Federal Employee
children.label: d .—Status of Member as Federal Employee
children.text: A member’s receipt of funds under this section or section 569 shall not conclusively determine for purposes of sections 202 through 209 of title 18 whether that member is an employee of the United States Government.
